ifc 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Format an IFC datetime as a string

Source: R/datetime.R
format.ifc_datetime.Rd

Converts an ifc_datetime to a character vector using a format string with IFC-aware tokens. Supports all tokens from format.ifc_date() plus:

Usage

# S3 method for class 'ifc_datetime'
format(x, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z", ...)

Arguments

x

An ifc_datetime vector.

format

Character string of format tokens. Default: "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z".

...

Ignored; included for S3 consistency.

Value

Character vector the same length as x.

Details

TokenMeaning
%HHour 00–23
%MMinute 00–59
%SSecond 00–60 (zero-padded)
%ZTimezone abbreviation (e.g. "EDT")
%zUTC offset (e.g. "+0000")

For Year Day and Leap Day, %B and %b produce "Year Day" or "Leap Day" respectively; %m, %d, %u, %a, %A produce empty strings. Time tokens are always applied normally.

Examples

x <- ifc_datetime("2024-07-14 09:30:00", tz = "UTC")
format(x)                       # "2024-07-14 09:30:00 UTC"
#> [1] "2024-07-27 09:30:00 UTC"
format(x, "%Y %B %d %H:%M:%S") # "2024 Sol 14 09:30:00"
#> [1] "2024 Sol 27 09:30:00"

On this page
